 `./scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts`

```text
1 // scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts
2 //
3 // Generates public/audio/phonemes.mp3 + phonemes.json from the IPA phonemes
4 // used in the AUS corpus. Uses eSpeak-NG (GPL v3 — audio output unrestricted)
5 // as the TTS backend, feeding Kirshenbaum phoneme codes directly so every
6 // clip is linguistically accurate rather than an "approximation map".
7 //
8 // Requires: espeak-ng, ffmpeg on PATH. Run: `yarn generate:phonemes`.
9 
10 import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process';
11 import {
12   existsSync,
13   mkdirSync,
14   readFileSync,
15   readdirSync,
16   rmSync,
17   writeFileSync,
18 } from 'node:fs';
19 import path from 'node:path';
20 import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
21 
22 const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
23 const repoRoot = path.join(__dirname, '..');
24 const corpusDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'src/data/words/curriculum/aus');
25 const outDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'public/audio');
26 const tmpDir = path.join(repoRoot, '.tmp/phoneme-sprite');
27 
28 const GAP_SEC = 0.25;
29 const SAMPLE_RATE = 22_050;
30 
31 /**
32  * IPA → Kirshenbaum ASCII phoneme codes for eSpeak-NG `[[...]]` input.
33  * Single consonants get a trailing schwa because stops are inaudible in
34  * isolation. Vowels and diphthongs speak alone. Clusters (ks, kw, juː) are
35  * multi-phoneme sequences the corpus treats as a single grapheme sound.
36  */
37 const IPA_TO_KIRSH: Record<string, string> = {
38   // Consonants
39   p: 'p',
40   b: 'b',
41   t: 't',
42   d: 'd',
43   k: 'k',
44   g: 'g',
45   f: 'f',
46   v: 'v',
47   s: 's',
48   z: 'z',
49   h: 'h',
50   θ: 'T',
51   ð: 'D',
52   ʃ: 'S',
53   ʒ: 'Z',
54   tʃ: 'tS',
55   dʒ: 'dZ',
56   m: 'm',
57   n: 'n',
58   ŋ: 'N',
59   l: 'l',
60   r: 'r',
61   w: 'w',
62   j: 'j',
63   // Multi-phoneme clusters the corpus stores as one grapheme `p` value
64   ks: 'ks',
65   kw: 'kw',
66   juː: 'ju:',
67   // Short vowels
68   e: 'E',
69   æ: '&',
70   ɒ: 'A',
71   ʌ: 'V',
72   ʊ: 'U',
73   ɪ: 'I',
74   ə: '@',
75   // Long vowels
76   iː: 'i:',
77   uː: 'u:',
78   ɑː: 'A:',
79   ɔː: 'O:',
80   ɜː: '3:',
81   // Diphthongs
82   aɪ: 'aI',
83   aʊ: 'aU',
84   eɪ: 'eI',
85   oʊ: 'oU',
86   ɔɪ: 'OI',
87   eə: 'e@',
88   ɛə: 'e@',
89   ɪə: 'I@',
90 };
91 
92 const CONSONANTS = new Set([
93   'p',
94   'b',
95   't',
96   'd',
97   'k',
98   'g',
99   'f',
100   'v',
101   's',
102   'z',
103   'h',
104   'θ',
105   'ð',
106   'ʃ',
107   'ʒ',
108   'tʃ',
109   'dʒ',
110   'm',
111   'n',
112   'ŋ',
113   'l',
114   'r',
115   'w',
116   'j',
117   'ks',
118   'kw',
119 ]);
120 
121 const buildKirshenbaumInput = (ipa: string): string => {
122   const code = IPA_TO_KIRSH[ipa];
123   if (!code) throw new Error(`No Kirshenbaum mapping for IPA "${ipa}"`);
124   return CONSONANTS.has(ipa) ? `[[${code}@]]` : `[[${code}]]`;
125 };
126 
127 const corpusPhonemes = (): string[] => {
128   const seen = new Set<string>();
129   for (const file of readdirSync(corpusDir).filter((f) =>
130     f.endsWith('.json'),
131   )) {
132     const raw = readFileSync(path.join(corpusDir, file), 'utf8');
133     const entries = JSON.parse(raw) as Array<{
134       graphemes?: Array<{ p: string }>;
135     }>;
136     for (const entry of entries) {
137       for (const g of entry.graphemes ?? []) {
138         if (g.p) seen.add(g.p);
139       }
140     }
141   }
142   return [...seen].toSorted();
143 };
144 
145 const probeDurationSec = (wav: string): number => {
146   const out = execFileSync(
147     'ffprobe',
148     [
149       '-v',
150       'error',
151       '-show_entries',
152       'format=duration',
153       '-of',
154       'default=nk=1:nw=1',
155       wav,
156     ],
157     { encoding: 'utf8' },
158   );
159   return Number(out.trim());
160 };
161 
162 const synthesizePhoneme = (ipa: string, outWav: string): void => {
163   const input = buildKirshenbaumInput(ipa);
164   execFileSync('espeak-ng', [
165     '-v',
166     'en',
167     '-s',
168     '140',
169     '-w',
170     outWav,
171     input,
172   ]);
173 };
174 
175 const main = (): void => {
176   if (existsSync(tmpDir))
177     rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
178   mkdirSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true });
179   mkdirSync(outDir, { recursive: true });
180 
181   const phonemes = corpusPhonemes();
182   const missing = phonemes.filter((p) => !IPA_TO_KIRSH[p]);
183   if (missing.length > 0) {
184     throw new Error(
185       `Corpus uses IPA phonemes with no Kirshenbaum mapping: ${missing.join(', ')}`,
186     );
187   }
188 
189   const silenceWav = path.join(tmpDir, 'silence.wav');
190   execFileSync('ffmpeg', [
191     '-y',
192     '-f',
193     'lavfi',
194     '-i',
195     `anullsrc=r=${SAMPLE_RATE}:cl=mono`,
196     '-t',
197     GAP_SEC.toString(),
198     silenceWav,
199   ]);
200 
201   interface SpriteEntry {
202     start: number;
203     duration: number;
204   }
205   const sprite: Record<string, SpriteEntry> = {};
206   const concatLines: string[] = [];
207   let cursor = 0;
208 
209   for (const [i, ipa] of phonemes.entries()) {
210     const safe = i.toString().padStart(3, '0');
211     const rawWav = path.join(tmpDir, `raw-${safe}.wav`);
212     const normWav = path.join(tmpDir, `norm-${safe}.wav`);
213     synthesizePhoneme(ipa, rawWav);
214     execFileSync('ffmpeg', [
215       '-y',
216       '-i',
217       rawWav,
218       '-ar',
219       SAMPLE_RATE.toString(),
220       '-ac',
221       '1',
222       normWav,
223     ]);
224     const dur = probeDurationSec(normWav);
225     sprite[ipa] = {
226       start: Math.round(cursor * 1000),
227       duration: Math.round(dur * 1000),
228     };
229     concatLines.push(`file '${normWav}'`, `file '${silenceWav}'`);
230     cursor += dur + GAP_SEC;
231   }
232 
233   const concatList = path.join(tmpDir, 'concat.txt');
234   writeFileSync(concatList, concatLines.join('\n'));
235 
236   const mp3Path = path.join(outDir, 'phonemes.mp3');
237   execFileSync('ffmpeg', [
238     '-y',
239     '-f',
240     'concat',
241     '-safe',
242     '0',
243     '-i',
244     concatList,
245     '-codec:a',
246     'libmp3lame',
247     '-qscale:a',
248     '6',
249     mp3Path,
250   ]);
251 
252   const jsonPath = path.join(outDir, 'phonemes.json');
253   writeFileSync(jsonPath, JSON.stringify(sprite, null, 2) + '\n');
254 
255   rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
256 
257   const totalMs = Math.round(cursor * 1000);
258   console.log(
259     `Generated ${phonemes.length} phoneme clips → ${path.relative(repoRoot, mp3Path)} (${totalMs}ms)`,
260   );
261 };
262 
263 main();
264
```

</details></tool-use>

commit 507137c4d802d5b35b9b06013818706c8e0ad1c5
Author: leocaseiro <leobok@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Apr 12 07:38:15 2026 +1000

    refactor(words): per-IPA phoneme sustain config + drop s loop gap
    
    Replaces the three category sets + three hard-coded filter chains with
    a single PHONEME_CONFIG map keyed by IPA. Each entry carries its own
    anchor vowel, silence-trim threshold, atempo stretch factor, fade ms,
    and loopable flag, so individual sounds can be tuned without touching
    the pipeline.
    
    Drops the 5 ms afade from every fricative so the WebAudio loop seam
    has no silence gap — fixes the audible "sss sss sss" cadence on the
    `s` chip. All other phoneme timings are unchanged.
    
    SKIP_UNIT=1 SKIP_STORYBOOK=1 SKIP_VR=1 SKIP_E2E=1 — iterating on sprite
    output one phoneme at a time per request; full suite will run once the
    tuning stabilises.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts b/scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts
index 1ca3b12..76fc39b 100644
--- a/scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts
+++ b/scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts
@@ -121,60 +121,133 @@ const CONSONANTS = new Set([
 ]);
 
 /**
- * Fricatives get synthesised bare and looped tight: the consonant is
- * already a steady-state hiss, so removing the schwa anchor keeps the
- * sustain sounding like "sssss" instead of "sssuuu".
+ * Per-phoneme sustain tuning. Every dial is exposed here so individual
+ * sounds can be adjusted without touching the rest of the corpus.
+ *
+ * - `anchor`: what follows the Kirshenbaum code inside `[[...]]`. Empty
+ *   for bare rendering (fricatives + vowels), `@` for the schwa release
+ *   sonorants need, `E` for the `/ɛ/` anchor plosives use so chips say
+ *   "teh"/"peh" instead of "tuh"/"puh".
+ * - `trimDb`: silenceremove threshold, or `null` to skip the trim pass.
+ *   Voiced fricatives peak very low so they need `-60`; vowels and
+ *   sonorants are fine at `-40`.
+ * - `stretch`: atempo multiplier (`1 | 2 | 4`). `4` chains two
+ *   atempo=0.5 passes; anything beyond that would need more chaining.
+ * - `fadeMs`: symmetric afade at both edges. Set to 0 to keep the loop
+ *   seam gap-free (cost: potential click, usually masked by noise).
+ * - `loopable`: marks the sprite entry so WebAudio wraps the segment
+ *   on hover-sustain.
  */
-const SUSTAINABLE_FRICATIVES = new Set<string>([

-  'v',
-  's',
-  'z',
-  'θ',
-  'ð',
-  'ʃ',
-  'ʒ',
-]);
+interface PhonemeConfig {
+  readonly anchor: '' | '@' | 'E';
+  readonly trimDb: number | null;
+  readonly stretch: 1 | 2 | 4;
+  readonly fadeMs: number;
+  readonly loopable: boolean;
+}
 
-/**
- * Nasals + liquids render as a brief burst when bare (eSpeak only emits
- * ~27 ms of actual consonant for `[[n]]`), so 4x stretching sounds choppy.
- * Keeping the `[[X@]]` anchor plus a 2x atempo stretch gives a smooth
- * ~650 ms release that matches the natural pronunciation the user asked
- * for ("n was better before"). These are NOT marked loopable since the
- * schwa tail would chant on loop — one playback per hover is enough.
- */

-  'ŋ',

```text
  'kw',
]);

/**
 * Per-phoneme sustain tuning. Every dial is exposed here so individual
 * sounds can be adjusted without touching the rest of the corpus.
 *
 * - `anchor`: what follows the Kirshenbaum code inside `[[...]]`. Empty
 *   for bare rendering (fricatives + vowels), `@` for the schwa release
 *   sonorants need, `E` for the `/ɛ/` anchor plosives use so chips say
 *   "teh"/"peh" instead of "tuh"/"puh".
 * - `trimDb`: silenceremove threshold, or `null` to skip the trim pass.
 *   Voiced fricatives peak very low so they need `-60`; vowels and
 *   sonorants are fine at `-40`.
 * - `stretch`: atempo multiplier (`1 | 2 | 4`). `4` chains two
 *   atempo=0.5 passes; anything beyond that would need more chaining.
 * - `fadeMs`: symmetric afade at both edges. Set to 0 to keep the loop
 *   seam gap-free (cost: potential click, usually masked by noise).
 * - `loopable`: marks the sprite entry so WebAudio wraps the segment
 *   on hover-sustain.
 */
interface PhonemeConfig {
  readonly anchor: '' | '@' | 'E';
  readonly trimDb: number | null;
  readonly stretch: 1 | 2 | 4;
  readonly fadeMs: number;
  readonly loopable: boolean;
}

const PHONEME_CONFIG: Record<string, PhonemeConfig> = {
  // Fricatives — bare, 4x stretch, no fade (noise masks seam clicks)
  s: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  z: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  f: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  v: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  θ: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  ð: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  ʃ: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  ʒ: { anchor: '', trimDb: -60, stretch: 4, fadeMs: 0, loopable: true },
  // Sonorants — schwa anchor, 2x stretch, natural release, non-looping
  m: {
    anchor: '@',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 20,
    loopable: false,
  },
  n: {
    anchor: '@',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 20,
    loopable: false,
  },
  ŋ: {
    anchor: '@',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 20,
    loopable: false,
  },
  l: {
    anchor: '@',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 20,
    loopable: false,
  },
  r: {
    anchor: '@',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 20,
    loopable: false,
  },
  // Sustainable short vowels — loopable so hover holds the vowel
  ɪ: {
    anchor: '',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 10,
    loopable: true,
  },
  æ: {
    anchor: '',
    trimDb: -40,
    stretch: 2,
    fadeMs: 10,
    loopable: true,
  },
};

const defaultAnchor = (ipa: string): '' | 'E' =>
  CONSONANTS.has(ipa) ? 'E' : '';

const configFor = (ipa: string): PhonemeConfig =>
  PHONEME_CONFIG[ipa] ?? {
    anchor: defaultAnchor(ipa),
    trimDb: null,
    stretch: 1,
    fadeMs: 0,
    loopable: false,
  };

const buildKirshenbaumInput = (ipa: string): string => {
  const code = IPA_TO_KIRSH[ipa];
  if (!code) throw new Error(`No Kirshenbaum mapping for IPA "${ipa}"`);
  return `[[${code}${configFor(ipa).anchor}]]`;
};

const buildFilterChain = (cfg: PhonemeConfig): string | null => {
  const parts: string[] = [];
  if (cfg.trimDb !== null) {
    parts.push(
      `silenceremove=start_periods=1:start_threshold=${cfg.trimDb}dB:` +
        `stop_periods=-1:stop_threshold=${cfg.trimDb}dB`,
    );
  }
  if (cfg.stretch === 2) parts.push('atempo=0.5');
  if (cfg.stretch === 4) parts.push('atempo=0.5', 'atempo=0.5');
  if (cfg.fadeMs > 0) {
    const d = (cfg.fadeMs / 1000).toFixed(3);
    parts.push(
      `afade=t=in:st=0:d=${d}`,
      'areverse',
      `afade=t=in:st=0:d=${d}`,
      'areverse',
    );
  }
  return parts.length > 0 ? parts.join(',') : null;
};

const corpusPhonemes = (): string[] => {
  const seen = new Set<string>();
  for (const file of readdirSync(corpusDir).filter((f) =>
    f.endsWith('.json'),
  )) {
    const raw = readFileSync(path.join(corpusDir, file), 'utf8');
    const entries = JSON.parse(raw) as Array<{
      graphemes?: Array<{ p: string }>;
    }>;
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 02:05:59Z)**_

Applied starting points for all 9 phonemes. **Restart the server** (`python3 tools/phoneme-tuner/app.py`) to load the new values.

## What I changed and why

| IPA | Change | Root cause |
|---|---|---|
| **s** | `stretch 4, fadeMs 0` (restored old PR) | You preferred the older, more-stretched loop |
| **f** | `stretch 2, gainDb +18, crop 0-70%, trim off` | `f` peaks at ~-50 dBFS (quietest fricative); needed big gain |
| **z** | `anchor @, stretch 2, gainDb +6, crop 0-60%` | 4× stretch on bare `[[z]]` killed the low-freq voicing → sounded like `s`. `@` anchor preserves voicing context |
| **h** | `bare, stretch 2, gainDb +15, crop 0-70%` | `V` anchor (ʌ) was louder than the aspiration; dropped anchor and boosted gain |
| **θ, ʃ, ʒ, ks** | `stretch 1.8, trim off, crop 10-80%, +6-10 dB` | **WSOLA zap artifact** — same as `f` earlier: heavy stretch on short HF noise warbles. Lower stretch + crop middle + gain fixes it |
| **ʒ** | added `anchor @` too | Voiced version of ʃ — needs buzz context like `z` |
| **kw** | `stretch 1.2, gainDb +8, crop 0-95%` | Clusters need almost no stretch; slight stretch + gain |

## Things to audition in the tuner

1. **`s`** — listen for the old sound, you said it was right
2. **`z`** — compare with `s`, should now have buzz
3. **`ʒ`** — compare with `ʃ`, same buzz test
4. **`θ/ʃ/ks`** — zap should be gone or much reduced
5. **`h`** — pure breathy /h/, no vowel

If any still sound off, use the tuner dials — all have undo. Common adjustments: push `gainDb` higher (safe up to ~+24), drop `stretch` closer to `1`, or tighten `crop` to skip artifact regions.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 02:12:06Z)**_

`PHONEME_CONFIG` **never reached master**. It only exists on the `feat/word-library-explorer` branch, introduced in commit `507137c` (`scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts`, lines ~120–250 of that revision).

Current state on master (`scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts:162-173`) is the _simpler_ generator — raw `[[code@]]` per consonant, no filter chain, no per-IPA config. That's why your master build sounds different: the old `PHONEME_CONFIG` tuning was committed on a feature branch that's still outstanding.

**To view/restore it:**

```bash
# read the old map
git show 507137c:scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts | sed -n '120,260p'

# or check out just that file into a scratch location
git show 507137c:scripts/generate-phoneme-sprite.ts > /tmp/old-phoneme-sprite.ts

# or check out the whole branch
git switch feat/word-library-explorer
```

**What the plan looks like from here:**

1. Finish tuning in the Phoneme Tuner → `tuned-config.json` is the authoritative source.
2. Hand that JSON to the sibling session so it can rewrite `PHONEME_CONFIG` (or its replacement) in `generate-phoneme-sprite.ts` on a fresh branch off master.
3. Re-run `yarn generate:phonemes` to produce `public/audio/phonemes.mp3` + `phonemes.json`.
4. Delete the stale `feat/word-library-explorer` branch once the new values land.

So: the tuner **replaces** the old `PHONEME_CONFIG` map. You're writing its successor right now.
